other_uses: Spiny-leaved evergreen shrub with golden flower clusters and dusty-blue tart berries; Oregon's state flower.
prep: Cook the very tart blue berries into jelly, wine, and juice (often mixed with sweeter fruit); the bright-yellow inner bark/root yields a dye and the medicinal alkaloid berberine. A handsome evergreen for shade.
soil: Dry to medium, well-drained soils; shade tolerant.
risks: Very tart raw; berberine root is medicinal, not food.
buy: Native-plant nurseries and native seed suppliers.
